page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m2.large is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the ecs.u2a server family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC cores running at a clock speed of 2.25 GHz. The instance does not utilize hyperthreading, providing a single thread per core. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, requiring network-attached storage for boot volumes and data persistence. Network connectivity is configured with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The hardware configuration presents a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, aligning it with compute-focused resource distributions for lightweight workloads.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and modest memory footprint, this instance is suitable for entry-level compute tasks, lightweight web serving, microservices, and basic utility workloads that require consistent CPU performance without the overhead of shared-core resource contention.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u2a-c1m2.large is an x86_64 virtual machine instance from the ecs.u2a family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores running at 2.25 GHz with a single thread per core. The instance provides 4.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It features no local storage and no GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Network performance is anchored by a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps. With its dedicated CPU allocation and compact memory footprint, this instance is designed for lightweight compute tasks, microservices, and basic utility workloads requiring stable, non-burstable CPU execution.
